title: CSR Registration Online in India | Process, Fees & Consultant

hero.headline: Get CSR Registration and Start Receiving CSR Funds
hero.subtext: Mandatory MCA registration for NGOs seeking CSR donations. Complete assistance from Professionals Law.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: CSR Registration is basically compulsory for all NGO, Trust or Society or Section 8 Company who wants CSR funding from Companies as per Companies Act-2013. As per the CSR Amendment Rules, 2021, implementation agencies are mandated to register themselves with the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) (by filing the Eform CSR-1) for getting a unique CSR Registration Number. This makes sure that only the verified and genuinely compliant players get CSR contributions. Professionals Law supports in the entire process of registration of CSR, which enables the registered entities to get money from corporate houses (fulfilling CSR requirements) to work on social development projects in association with such corporate houses.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: CSR Registration is a "MANDATE" for NGOs, not for profit associations and trusts etc to legitimately get CSR Share from Corporates under Section 135 of the Companies Act. It encourages responsibility, openness and credibility amongst the implementation partners within the CSR ecosystem. Even if the NGOs are eligible, they can’t legally be part of the CSR program of companies without being registered under CSR. Professionals Law checks the status with the MCA that your organization is registered and does not forget to fulfill any norm, it will make you eligible for CSR donations across India from leading corporates.

criteria: Must be registered as a Section 8 Company, Trust, or Society
Must have an active 12A and 80G certificate (for tax exemption benefits)
Should be operational for at least 3 years
Must have an active bank account in the NGO’s name

body.title: Eligibility for CSR Funds:
body.description: Registered NGOs become eligible to receive CSR funds from companies obligated under the Companies Act.

body.title: Enhanced Credibility:
body.description: CSR Registration boosts the trustworthiness of the NGO among donors, corporates, and other stakeholders.

body.title: Government Recognition:
body.description: The NGO is officially recognized by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs as a valid implementation agency.

body.title: Increased Funding Opportunities:
body.description: Opens doors for multiple collaborations with corporates across industries seeking reliable CSR partners.

body.title: Transparency and Compliance:
body.description: Ensures legal and regulatory compliance, preventing future disqualifications or funding blocks.

documents: Certificate of Registration (Trust Deed / Society MOA / Section 8 COI)
Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) of Authorized Representative
NGO Darpan ID (recommended but not mandatory)

body.description: Professionals Law collects all required documents from the NGO
DSC of the authorized signatory is prepared if not available
Application is filed online on the MCA portal
MCA verifies submitted data and documents
MCA issues a unique CSR Registration Number
Professionals Law assists with project proposal alignment and reporting practices

body.title: Approval & CSR Registration Number Issuance
body.description: 5 to 7 working days (subject to MCA processing)

body.title: Total Duration with Professionals Law
body.description: Usually completed within 7 to 10 working days

body.title: Incorrect or false information in the CSR-1 form may lead to cancellation of registration
Non-compliance can result in blacklisting by MCA or revocation of registration
Companies may avoid unregistered NGOs, leading to loss of funding opportunities
Penalties under the Companies Act, 2013, for both companies and implementing agencies for violating CSR provisions

body.question: Is CSR registration mandatory for NGOs?
body.answer: Yes, it is mandatory for any NGO seeking CSR funds from companies under the Companies Act, 2013.

body.question: What is Form CSR-1?
body.answer: It is the application form prescribed by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs for registering as a CSR implementing agency.

body.question: Can a new NGO apply for CSR registration?
body.answer: Only NGOs operational for at least 3 years and having 12A & 80G registration can apply.

body.question: What is the CSR Registration Number?
body.answer: It is a unique ID issued by MCA to registered NGOs eligible to receive CSR funds.

body.question: Is it compulsory to have 12A & 80G registration?
body.answer: Yes, it is mandatory to qualify as an eligible CSR implementing agency.

body.question: How long does it take to obtain CSR registration?
body.answer: Usually within 7 to 10 working days with complete documentation and proper filing.

body.question: What if the application is rejected?
body.answer: Professionals Law helps in rectifying the errors and re-submitting the application.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist in project proposal creation?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law helps NGOs in aligning proposals according to corporate CSR requirements.

body.question: Can a CSR registration be revoked?
body.answer: Yes, if the organization fails to comply with rules or submits fraudulent information, registration may be cancelled by the MCA.
